identity.short_name: 240^\circ overlap volume from two quadratic caps

subdomain(s): intersection volume of solids of revolution
piecewise integration from min/max of radii

problem_spec.summary: Two planar regions bounded by y=0 and quadratic graphs (both above the x-axis) are each rotated through a fixed angle theta about the x-axis. The intersection of the two resulting partial solids of revolution has volume V, computed via cross-sectional disk sectors whose radius is the pointwise minimum of the two quadratics on the overlap x-interval. A final engineered expression of the form (K*pi)/(m*V) - C is evaluated.
givens: Region I: 0 <= y <= f(x) on an interval [x1,x2], with f a quadratic nonnegative on [x1,x2].
Region II: 0 <= y <= g(x) on an interval [x3,x4], with g a quadratic nonnegative on [x3,x4].
S_I is obtained by rotating Region I about the x-axis through angle theta.
S_II is obtained by rotating Region II about the x-axis through angle theta (same theta and same angular sector).
W = S_I ∩ S_II is the intersection solid and has volume V.
A final expression E = (K*pi)/(m*V) - C is requested (constants K,m,C given in the statement).
ask: Compute the exact value of E.

parameter(s).name: f
parameter(s).purpose: upper boundary above the x-axis for Region I

parameter(s).name: g
parameter(s).purpose: upper boundary above the x-axis for Region II

parameter(s).name: intervals
parameter(s).purpose: controls the overlap interval where both solids exist and where min(f,g) is evaluated

parameter(s).name: theta
parameter(s).purpose: fraction of full revolution for each solid

parameter(s).name: engineered_constants
parameter(s).purpose: chooses K,m,C so E evaluates to a small exact integer after substituting V

invariant(s).name: partial_rotation_scaling
invariant(s).statement: Rotating a planar region about the x-axis through angle theta produces disk-sector (or washer-sector) cross-sections whose area is scaled by factor theta/(2*pi) relative to a full 2*pi revolution.

invariant(s).name: overlap_cross_section_min_radius
invariant(s).statement: On the x-overlap interval [max(x1,x3), min(x2,x4)], the cross-section of the intersection solid W at position x is a disk-sector of radius r(x)=min(f(x), g(x)); outside the overlap interval the cross-section area is 0.

essential_assumption(s): f(x) and g(x) are nonnegative on their stated intervals so radii are valid without absolute values.
The two partial rotations share the same angular sector, so their intersection cross-section uses the same theta/(2*pi) scaling.
The interval overlap is nonempty; otherwise V=0 is still uniquely determined.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Given explicit quadratics f and g and explicit intervals, the overlap interval is fixed and the breakpoint set where f(x)=g(x) is finite and computable. This yields a unique piecewise-polynomial radius r(x)=min(f(x),g(x)), hence a unique volume V from a definite integral of a fully determined area function. Substituting V into E gives a unique real number; exact simplification yields a unique integer output.

technique(s).name: verify_nonnegativity_and_overlap
technique(s).purpose: confirm the radii are nonnegative and determine the effective x-interval where both solids exist

technique(s).name: locate_switch_points
technique(s).purpose: solve f(x)=g(x) to determine subintervals on which min(f,g) is represented by a single quadratic

technique(s).name: build_piecewise_min_area_and_integrate
technique(s).purpose: build the scaled cross-sectional area A(x) using r(x)=min(f(x),g(x)) and integrate to obtain V

theorem(s).name: volume_by_cross_sections
theorem(s).role: V = ∫ A(x) dx for solids with known cross-sectional area A(x)

theorem(s).name: polynomial_integration
theorem(s).role: exact antiderivatives for polynomials (hence for squared quadratics)

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: reduce a 3D intersection volume to a piecewise definite integral via cross-sections

critical_trick(s): Model the overlap (intersection) of two partial solids as a disk-sector with radius min(f(x),g(x)) at each x.
Find the unique switch point solving f(x)=g(x) to split the integral into polynomial pieces.
Use engineered constants so (K*pi)/(m*V) - C simplifies to an integer.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: exact symbolic solving for breakpoints and exact integration/simplification with rational numbers and pi

import_hint(s): from sympy import symbols, integrate, pi, Rational, simplify, solve

task(s).name: verify_breakpoints
task(s).reason: solve f(x)=g(x) and confirm the ordering (which function is smaller) on each subinterval

task(s).name: verify_volume_exact
task(s).reason: integrate the piecewise area and simplify the result to an exact multiple of pi

task(s).name: verify_final_integer
task(s).reason: compute E symbolically and confirm it reduces to an integer
